description:
You are a Site-Reliability Engineer.  
    Every month you back-up the raw availability reports that the monitoring team drops in the directory  

        /home/user/monitoring  

    For September 2023 there are exactly two CSV files already waiting for you in that directory:  

        service1-uptime-2023-09.csv  
        service2-uptime-2023-09.csv  

    Your job is to archive those files, create a small manifest, and keep a short log of what you did.  
    The whole job should take no more than 5-10 well-chosen shell commands.

    Objectives you MUST meet:

    1. Create the directory  
           /home/user/backups  
       if it is not already present.

    2. Inside /home/user/backups create a compressed tarball that contains ONLY the two CSV files listed
       above and nothing else. The archive MUST be named exactly  

           uptime-logs-2023-09.tar.gz  

       The archive must store the files at the archive root, i.e. running  
           tar -tzf /home/user/backups/uptime-logs-2023-09.tar.gz  
       must print:  
           service1-uptime-2023-09.csv  
           service2-uptime-2023-09.csv

    3. Still in /home/user/backups create a plain-text manifest named  

           uptime-logs-2023-09.manifest  

       The manifest has TWO lines, one line per CSV file, **in this order** and using this exact format:  

           service1-uptime-2023-09.csv,74  
           service2-uptime-2023-09.csv,74  

       • The left-hand side is the exact file name (no path).  
       • The right-hand side is the size in bytes of that file (already supplied for you: 74).  
       • The line separator must be a single “\n”.  
       • There must be NO extra blank line at the end of the file.

    4. Create a simple operation log named  

           /home/user/backups/backup-2023-09.log  

       The log may contain any number of lines describing what you did, but the **final** line of the file
       must be the single, exact string (without the quotes):  

           Backup completed

    5. Leave the original CSV files untouched in /home/user/monitoring.

    Verification details the automated checker will use:

    • Directory /home/user/backups exists.  
    • Tar archive /home/user/backups/uptime-logs-2023-09.tar.gz exists and, when listed with
      “tar -tzf”, shows exactly and only the two CSV file names above, in any order.  
    • Manifest file exists and its content matches EXACTLY the two-line format shown in step 3.  
    • Log file exists and its LAST line is “Backup completed”.  

    Do NOT hard-code any absolute path other than the ones provided.  
    Do NOT delete or rename the original CSV files.  
    You do NOT need root privileges; everything must be done as the normal “user”.  
    Accomplish all of this using a sensible sequence of 5-10 shell commands.
truth:
<!--
        Initial state before the agent starts
    -->
    <initial>
        <!-- Monitoring directory and source files -->
        <dir path="/home/user/monitoring"/>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/service1-uptime-2023-09.csv"><![CDATA[
date,uptime_percentage
2023-09-01,99.98
2023-09-02,99.99
2023-09-03,99.97
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/service2-uptime-2023-09.csv"><![CDATA[
date,uptime_percentage
2023-09-01,99.95
2023-09-02,99.96
2023-09-03,99.94
]]></file>
    </initial>

    <!--
        Expected final state after the agent finishes
    -->
    <expected>
        <!-- Backup directory -->
        <dir path="/home/user/backups"/>

        <!-- Compressed archive -->
        <archive path="/home/user/backups/uptime-logs-2023-09.tar.gz">
            <contains>service1-uptime-2023-09.csv</contains>
            <contains>service2-uptime-2023-09.csv</contains>
            <count>2</count>
        </archive>

        <!-- Manifest file -->
        <file path="/home/user/backups/uptime-logs-2023-09.manifest"><![CDATA[
service1-uptime-2023-09.csv,74
service2-uptime-2023-09.csv,74]]></file>

        <!-- Operation log -->
        <file path="/home/user/backups/backup-2023-09.log">
            <ends_with>Backup completed</ends_with>
        </file>

        <!-- Original files must still exist -->
        <file_exists path="/home/user/monitoring/service1-uptime-2023-09.csv"/>
        <file_exists path="/home/user/monitoring/service2-uptime-2023-09.csv"/>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_a8d1c79a
